title: Rocket Ship Bookcase
summary: A kids' bookcase shaped like a rocket — jigsaw-cut fins and a beveled nose cone turn a plain shelf tower into a themed piece.
description:
Underneath the theming, this is a standard shelf tower — two sides and four shelves — with three details doing all the visual work: jigsaw-cut fins nailed to the outside, a beveled two-piece roof that comes to a point like a nose cone, and a plywood back cut to trace the whole rocket silhouette rather than a plain rectangle. Building the box first and adding the rocket details after is what keeps this manageable; the shelf tower itself is beginner-friendly, and the rocket shape is trim, not structure.

The roof is the one part that needs real care: it's two pieces beveled at 45° on their top edge only, meeting at a ridge, so dry-fit both before nailing to make sure the point closes cleanly. Trace the plywood back directly off the assembled bookcase rather than trusting a fixed dimension — the exact rocket outline depends on your actual fin and roof placement.

tools.note: Cuts the rocket fins and traces the plywood back to the assembled shape.
Cuts the 45° bevel on the two roof pieces.

cutList.note: Jigsaw a fin profile before attaching.
Long point to long point, both ends beveled 45°, NOT parallel.
Long point to point measurement; only the ridge end is beveled 45°, the tail cut is square.
Trace and cut to the assembled bookcase's actual rocket silhouette.

steps.title: Build the shelf tower
steps.body: Attach the four shelves to the two sides using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, 2" self-tapping screws, or 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Cut and attach the fins
steps.body: Cut the two rocket fins out of the 1x8 with a jigsaw. Fasten from the inside of the bookcase with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue (pocket holes from the back side into the outside of the shelf work too), keeping the bottoms flush with the bookcase's bottom edge.

steps.title: Attach the footer
steps.body: Fasten the footer with nails and glue. If you want to cut a decorative flame shape out of the footer, plan your nail placement first so you don't cut through a nail when shaping it afterward.

steps.title: Attach the top shelf
steps.body: Set your miter saw to a 45° bevel and cut the top shelf piece to a 19-1/2" long-point-to-long-point measurement. Fasten it with a 3/4" overhang on the ends, keeping the front and back edges flush.

steps.title: Build and attach the roof
steps.body: Cut the two roof pieces to a 15" long-point-to-long-point measurement, with only the ridge end beveled at 45° — the tail cut stays square. Dry-fit both pieces against each other and the top shelf before nailing; a gap at the ridge is the most visible mistake this build can make. Attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Trace and attach the back
steps.body: Set the assembled bookcase on the 1/4" plywood sheet, trace the outline in pencil, remove it, and cut along the line with a jigsaw. Attach the traced back panel with 3/4" brad nails and glue into every board it crosses.

images.alt: Kids' bookcase shaped like a rocket ship with fins and a nose-cone roof
